title: Rootless run fails
body:
> /kind bug

**Description**

`/usr/bin/podman --log-level=debug run hello-world` fails, [logs here](http://home.in.tum.de/~engelke/raw/podman.1.log)

**Describe the results you received:**

See logs.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Some hello world message.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.6.5
Go Version:    go1.10.3
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

(as included in Silverblue; version 28.20180707.0)

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  MemFree: 113836032
  MemTotal: 4015460352
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: localhost.localdomain
  kernel: 4.17.3-200.fc28.x86_64
  os: linux
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 13
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: []
  GraphRoot: /home/aengelke/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000/run

```

comments.body:
@giuseppe PTAL when you get a chance.

I tried reproducing locally, and I'm running into issues pulling images while rootless - might be another bug there.

right that doesn't work on Silverlight as `/home` is a symlink to `var/home`.

The error is coming from runc that doesn't like symlinks into the path to the container rootfs:
```
/home/aengelke/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs/dir/86037da8b7681a729869f5478a543655d79d9bd7aab5c8de0c639caee073b6a6 is not an absolute path or is a symlink
```
I am taking a look, should be enough to fully resolve the path before feeding it to the runtime
Thanks for tracking this down -- my temporary workaround is to set `HOME=/var/home/aengelke` instead.

does that solve the issue for you?  Do you encounter other issues after the workaround?

@mheon what images have you tried?  Oner issue I am aware of is with images that have device nodes included in the layers will fail to be extracted, as a non root user cannot use `mknod`, but that should be addressed by the kernel in Linux 4.18

@aengelke I've opened a PR: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1075

It solved the problem for me on Fedora Atomic (Version: 28.20180708.0 (2018-07-08 20:03:31), but if you are still having other issues, I can address them as part of the same PR

With the workaround the hello world image works fine. However, running `fedora:28` (or `registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:28`, or `debian`) fails, [logs here](http://home.in.tum.de/~engelke/raw/podman.2.log). This might be the issue that  @mheon  is seeing.
@aengelke is your username listed in /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid?
Uhh, no, it's not. What shall I put there?
Ok, after putting some generous amount of subuid and subgids, it works. Is this the intended behavior and just missing some documentation? Thanks anyway.

the subuids/subgids should be added automatically by `adduser`, but that doesn't work if your are using some old `/etc` configuration files that were generated before the `adduser` supported the subids (I also had the same problem with my ancient configurations files and I needed to add the mappings manually).

I think we should make it an error and don't let podman run if there are not subids available for the user.  The only blocker for it is our CI.
@giuseppe I'm not sure exactly what was happening on my end, but deleting/recreating the c/storage dir in my home directory seems to have fixed it.
@rhatdan Don't think so, nothing in the audit logs. Maybe regular old Unix permissions were messed up somewhere?
@giuseppe I think we need to talk about having a storage.conf for non root users, and then users could customize it by copying it to their homedir.
@mheon Maybe.  Might have been blocked by SELinux dontaudit rules also.  But who knows.

@rhatdan I relaxed the error for podman when there are no entries in /etc/subuid, /etc/subgid so that tests could pass on the CI.  But I think we should re-enable it, or allow it only from the CI, as running with only one user mapped breaks almost any image
